site.name: Clodsite
site.purpose: A governed website-building workflow that interviews you, produces a reviewable spec, and deploys to Cloudflare Pages — compiling deterministic steps into scripts and reserving Claude inference for the steps that actually require reasoning.
site.audience: Developers and technical users who want to build and deploy websites quickly

pages.title: Home
pages.purpose: Sell Clodsite as a quick and simple way to a solid website foundation.
pages.content_outline: Hero with punchy headline and the five-command workflow (git clone → /setup → /interview → /plan → /build → /deploy). Briefly introduce the hybrid [SCRIPT]/[LLM] architecture concept. Tone: your trusted friend showing you how it's done. CTA to Getting Started.

pages.title: About
pages.purpose: Explain the Clodsite thesis: much of what we lean on AI for can and should be replaced with deterministic scripts.
pages.content_outline: The core argument: English is a comfortable programming language but an expensive one to execute repeatedly. Every LLM call costs tokens; deterministic operations (read a file, check a directory, run a command) belong in scripts. Introduce the [SCRIPT]/[LLM]/[HYBRID] annotation scheme. Two benefits: determinism and cost. Brief author note: 40+ year software career, distilling hard-won discipline about what belongs in inference vs. deterministic code.

pages.title: Getting Started
pages.purpose: Show developers how to use Clodsite from clone to live site.
pages.content_outline: Prerequisites: Claude Code installed, Cloudflare account. The five commands with brief description of each: /setup, /interview, /plan, /build, /deploy. Show the terminal snippet: git clone → cd → claude → /setup → /interview → /plan → /build → /deploy. Link to GitHub repo at github.com/nopolabs/clodsite. Friendly, knowledgeable-friend tone.

pages.title: Contact
pages.purpose: Provide a way for visitors to get in touch.
pages.content_outline: Simple, minimal contact page. Email: contact@clodsite.com. Short line about what kinds of messages are welcome: questions, feedback, collaboration ideas.
